folio.label: 6v
folio.l: Twenty bokes clad , in blak and in rede

note.notetxt: 296 there is a sign on the left margin like a 'chiave di violino'. Looks like same ink. It seems an imitation of the paraph, or simply a decoration in ink ODR

folio.l: Of Aristotle , and his philosophie
Than riche robes , or fethil or sautrie
But al be it though , he were a Philosophre
ñet had he but litel , gold in his coffre
But al æat he , from his frendis hent
On bokes and on lernyng , he it spent
And bisily gan , for the Soules pray

note.notetxt: Soules303 s is a long s; the stem is made by a forking assender from the feet to the head into two strokes. Probably indication of capital letter as all the capital in this hand have otious strokes in loops ODR

folio.l: Of hem , æ ©af hi~ , wherwith to scoley
Of stody tok he , most cure and hede
Nou©t a word spak he , more than was nede
And æ was seide , in foåme and reuäence
And short and quyk , & ful of heigh sentence
Sownyng´ in moral vätue , was his speche
And gladly wold he lerne , and gladly teche
A Sergeau~t of lawe , there was , war & wys
That ful often had ben , at Parvys
That was ful riche , of excellence
Discrete he was , and of gret reuäence
He semed swicú , his wordes were so wyse
Iustice had he be , ful often in assise

note.notetxt: wyse316 the final e has a flowrishing at the end of the top loop. This rhyme with the following assise, where the e is traced in the same way. The final e of excellence is traced with a flowrishing which depart from the tail of the loop. It seems that there is a visual alternation between the way the two e are traced.ODR

folio.l: Bothe by patent , and pleyn comissiou~
For his science , and his heigh renou~
Of fees and robes , had he many on
So grete a purchasoå , was nowher non
Al was fee simple , to hi~ in effect´
His påchasyng , myght nat ben , in suspect´
Nowher so besy aman . as he there nas
And ©et he semed , besiere than he was
In tämes had he cas , and domes alle
That fro æe tyme , of kyng wiÂ , weren falle
Ther to he coude endyte , and make a thyng´
There coude noman pynche , at his writyng´
And euäy statut coude he , pleynly by rote
He rod but homely , in a Medlee Cote

note.notetxt: Medlee330 M is traced as a coursive m with the minims attached not traced separately. Indication of Capitals?.ODR

folio.l: Girt w a seynt of silk , w barres smale
Of his array , telle I , no lengere tale
A Frankeleyn æäe was , in this compaignye
Whyt was his berd , as is the daysye
Of his complexion , he was sangweyn
Wel loued he by the morwe , a soppe in wyn
To lyue in delyt , was euäe his wo~ne
